title: Egypt
description: Test your knowledge of ancient Egypt!

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was _unique_ about the way the Nile River flooded in ancient Egypt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The result of rich soil from flooding was not unique to the Nile River.

choices.content: It was so regular and predictable that the Egyptian seasons were based around it
Flooding made the soil next to the Nile rich and fertile
The Nile flooded every several weeks

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which best explains the purpose of an Old Kingdom pyramid?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Hint: Old Kingdom pyramids were not used as defensive military towers.

choices.content: It housed the body of a dead Egyptian king and his retainers
It was intended for grain storage, to demonstrate a king's generosity and wisdom
It was created as a lookout point so that military leaders could be aware of invaders

hints: Hieroglyphs are not related to embalming.

choices.content: a written form of the Egyptian language.
a ritual application of the nitre used to embalm a mummy
the high-water mark of the Nile's floodplain

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is an example of *divine kingship*, as practiced by the rulers of Egypt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Leaders of the Zhou dynasty in China created the idea of the Mandate of Heaven; it was not used by Egyptian rulers.

choices.content: A king or pharaoh associating themselves with a god like Amun-Ra or Horus
Claiming the right to rule under the Mandate of Heaven
The children of kings were thought to inherit divine qualities that were stronger than those of their parents

assessment_items.question_content:
**What do the Middle Kingdom Egyptian rulers’ maintenance of standing armies and fortifications tell us?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Egyptian leaders wanted to take power away from regional governments.

choices.content: the Egyptian government’s ability to consolidate power and control resources
Egyptian citizens’ sense of duty to serve their empire
a shift away from Egyptian leaders wanting regional governments to flourish
